response.justification: Bogdan Szabo's career is built almost entirely on TypeScript, Node.js, and web/mobile technologies, with only a brief 6-month stint writing C/C++ components at National Instruments (2014-2015) and a C++ bachelor thesis project. This falls drastically short of the 5-10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows required. He has no fixed income, fixed income derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge in any technically relevant capacity; his 15-month role at RIDE Capital involved Node.js and GraphQL, not systems-level C++ in a financial context. There is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle experience, no Windows platform focus, and no exposure to Summit, STL, Kafka/MQ, or financial instruments that the role demands.
strengths: Demonstrated CI/CD and DevOps discipline across multiple roles (GitLab, Docker, trunk-based development, Altom)
Brief but real C++ exposure at National Instruments writing components for an engineering IDE
Some investment-adjacent domain context from RIDE Capital engagement
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (roughly 6 months professional + a thesis) versus the 5-10 years mandatory requirement
Zero fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets technical domain knowledge; primary domain is web and direct mail automation
No evidence of Windows platform development, SQL Server/Oracle, or any capital-markets tooling (Summit, Kafka, MQ)
